audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's final oath/defense: covenant with eyes, integrity scales, justice toward servants, poor, widow, orphan, refusal of wealth-idolatry, hospitality, transparency, land ethics, and closing of Job's words.

verses.restored: I made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I gaze upon a young woman?
For what would be the portion of the Divine from above, or the inheritance of Shaddai from on high?
Is not calamity for the unjust, and disaster for workers of harm?
Does he not see my ways and count all my steps?
If I have walked with falsehood, and my foot has hurried to deceit,
let him weigh me in scales of justice, and let the Divine know my integrity.
If my step has turned from the way, and my heart walked after my eyes, and any stain has clung to my palms,
then let me sow and another eat, and let what grows for me be rooted out.
If my heart has been enticed toward a woman, and I have lain in wait at my neighbor’s doorway,
then let my woman grind for another, and let others bow over her.
For that would be a shameful act, and it would be an iniquity for judges.
For it is a fire that consumes to Abaddon and would root out all my increase.
If I rejected the case of my male servant or my female servant when they contended with me,
what then would I do when the Divine rises? When he visits, what would I answer him?
Did not the One who made me in the womb make him? Did not one form us in the womb?
If I withheld the desire of the poor, or caused the eyes of the widow to fail,
or ate my morsel alone, and the orphan did not eat from it,
for from my youth he grew up with me as with a father, and from my mother’s womb I guided her,
if I saw anyone perishing without clothing, or a needy one without covering,
if his loins did not bless me, and he was not warmed with the fleece of my sheep,
if I raised my hand against the orphan because I saw my help in the gate,
then let my shoulder fall from its shoulder blade, and my arm be broken from its socket.
For calamity from the Divine was dread to me, and because of his majesty I could not do such things.
If I made gold my confidence, or called fine gold my trust,
if I rejoiced because my wealth was great and because my hand had found much,
if I saw the light when it shone, or the moon moving in splendor,
and my heart was secretly enticed, and my hand kissed my mouth,
this also would be an iniquity for judges, for I would have denied the Divine above.
If I rejoiced at the ruin of one who hated me, or exulted when evil found him,
I did not allow my mouth to sin by asking for his life with a curse.
If the people of my tent did not say, Who can find one not filled with his meat?
The sojourner did not lodge outside; I opened my doors to the wayfarer.
If I covered my transgressions like Adam/humanity, hiding my guilt in my bosom,
because I feared the great multitude, or the contempt of clans terrified me, so that I kept silence and did not go out of the door,
Oh, that I had one to hear me! Look, my mark/signature. Let Shaddai answer me, and let my accuser write a document.
Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; I would bind it on me like a crown.
I would declare to him the number of my steps; like a prince I would approach him.
If my ground cries out against me, and its furrows weep together,
if I have eaten its strength without silver, or caused the soul of its owners to breathe out,
let thorns grow instead of wheat, and stinkweed instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.
